title: sarothamnus scoparius subsp. maritimus
intro: Sarothamnus scoparius subsp. maritimus, also known as maritime gorse, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the pea family (Fabaceae). This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which are derived from its alkaloids and flavonoids. The health benefits of maritime gorse include reducing inflammation and improving digestive issues, making it a popular herbal remedy for various ailments. Maritime gorse is often used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be made by infusing the plant's flowers, leaves, or stems in hot water or a solvent like ethanol.
